meta.reportLabel: Mon - the war-shock gap HELD but is FADING: WTI ~$74 (from $71.2 Fri), DXY ~101.1, but gold FAILED the haven bid (-1.2% to ~$4,070). US ran a 4th strike Sun, yet Iran-Oman mediation is live and Oman drafted a two-corridor Strait proposal; Trump says truce talks continue. Regime rotates from oil-shock to WAIT-FOR-CPI: Tue Jul 14 US June CPI (headline soft ~3.9%, core firm ~2.9%) is now the driver. Yen's haven bounce never came - carry re-grinds USD/JPY ~162. Cleanest lean still short EUR/USD.
meta.coverage: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, AUD, NZD, CAD · 18 pairs + DXY + JPYBASKET + GER40 + XAU + XAG + USOIL
meta.regime: The Sunday war-shock gap materialised but is already fading, and the board rotates from risk-off oil-shock toward wait-for-CPI. Markets opened to the expected moves - WTI ~$74 (from $71.2 Fri), DXY ~101.1 - but the panic did not extend: the US ran a 4th strike Sunday, yet Iran-Oman mediation is live, Oman drafted a concrete two-corridor Strait proposal (Southern/Omani lane free, Northern/Iranian lane open but needing Iran approval), and Trump signalled the truce talks continue. The tell is gold, which FAILED its haven bid and fell ~1.2% to ~$4,070. So oil is elevated but capped, the haven trade is unwinding, and the dominant driver flips back to Tue Jul 14 US CPI (headline soft ~3.9%, core firm ~2.9%). The yen's haven bounce never came, so carry re-grinds USD/JPY back toward ~162.
meta.nextBigEvent: Tue Jul 14, 12:30 UTC US June CPI - the dominant near-term driver: headline seen soft (-0.1% m/m -> ~3.9%) vs core firm (+0.3%, ~2.9%), a split print and a genuine two-way for the dollar. Geopolitics tail: an Oman two-corridor deal (or a breakdown) re-prices oil/haven in minutes.

dailyRead: The Sunday war-shock gap materialised - but it is already FADING, and the board rotates from 'risk-off oil-shock' toward 'wait-for-CPI.' Markets opened to the expected moves - WTI gapped to ~$74 (from $71.2 Friday, +5.4% on the week), DXY firmed to ~101.1 - but the panic has NOT extended: the US ran a 4th strike Sunday, yet Iran-Oman mediation is live and Oman has now drafted a concrete two-corridor Strait proposal (Southern/Omani-waters lane free under pre-war rules; Northern/Iranian-waters lane open but needing Iran's prior approval, no tolls), and Trump signalled the truce talks continue even through the strikes. The tell is gold, which did NOT hold its haven bid - it fell ~1.2% to ~$4,070 as the de-escalation path + a firm dollar capped it. So oil is elevated but rangebound, the haven trade is unwinding at the margin, and the dominant near-term driver flips back to Tuesday's US June CPI (headline seen soft, -0.1% m/m -> ~3.9% on June's lower gasoline; core firm +0.3% -> ~2.9%) - a split print that is a genuine two-way for the dollar. The yen's would-be haven bounce never arrived (risk-off faded, gold sold), so JPY stays weak and carry re-grinds USD/JPY back up around ~162. Cleanest lean: still short EUR/USD (energy-importer at a 1-yr low into a firm-USD, higher-for-longer tape), sized modestly ahead of CPI; oil is a two-way now (contested-Strait floor vs Oman-proposal cap), and every geopolitics call is one headline from reversing.

today.headline: Gold FAILED the war-shock bid - fell ~1.2% to ~$4,070 as fear premium unwinds
today.read: The Sunday haven bid never held: de-escalation hope (Oman talks) + a firm USD capped gold and turned it lower. Downgraded to RANGE (LOW) - the clearest sign the market is pricing de-escalation, not escalation. A geopolitics re-escalation or a soft US CPI (lower real rates) re-arms it.

today.headline: The haven bounce never came - carry re-grinds USD/JPY back toward ~162
today.read: Risk-off faded and gold sold, so the pivotal yen haven bounce resolved AGAINST the yen; carry + Japan's energy bill re-grind USD/JPY up toward 162-163 and the MoF zone. USD/JPY RANGE->long tilt. A re-escalation finally triggers the haven bid; a >163 grind forces the MoF's hand.

today.headline: Firm ~101.1 but the bid rotates from HAVEN to RATES into CPI
today.read: DXY holds ~101.1 on higher-for-longer, but the war-shock haven premium is deflating (gold faded, oil capped). Jul 14 CPI is the reset: headline soft (~3.9%) vs core firm (~2.9%), a genuine two-way. A soft headline misread dovish cracks DXY under 100.5; a hot core re-fires the no-cut trade.

today.headline: Oil bid persists (WTI ~$74) but the Oman cap makes it modest
today.read: The terms-of-trade bid holds USD/CAD off ~1.42, but the two-corridor proposal caps oil so it is a modest tailwind, not the Sunday supply-shock. USD/CAD SHORT (LOW-MED). BoC sidelined Jul 15; an Oman deal crushes oil and the CAD bid; a hot US CPI re-fires USD/CAD.

today.headline: Claws back risk-beta loss as the war-shock fades
today.read: With the panic easing AUD recovers the Sunday risk-off leg, lightly helped by LNG-export status; sticky-core floor, thin Aug tail (~36%). MIXED->firming. A geopolitics re-escalation re-softens it; a hot Q2 CPI (late Jul) re-arms the hawks.

today.headline: Still the 1-yr-low laggard, but the capped oil eases the acute hit
today.read: EUR stays the energy-importer laggard at ~1.14 vs a firm USD, though the capped oil move eases the terms-of-trade pain. Short-EUR/USD remains the cleanest lean (MED), sized down into CPI. A soft US CPI relieves it; a hawkish ECB Jul 23 core tone hands it a leg.

today.headline: Down-gap threat recedes as risk steadies; dovish-hike floor holds
today.read: The peaked-3.9%/higher-hurdle floor stands and, with risk-off fading, the high-beta down-gap threat recedes. NZ Q2 CPI ~Jul 16 is the swing. A fresh geopolitics leg re-hits NZD; a >4% CPI re-arms the hawks.

today.headline: No change - firm own rate story as risk-off fades
today.read: Services CPI 3.7% keeps both hike dissents alive into Jul 30; with the war-shock fading GBP trades its own firm rate story rather than as a risk passenger. A hot US CPI drags cable via the dollar; UK June CPI (~Jul 22) is the check.

strength.tag: DXY ~101.1; bid rotates haven -> rates into CPI
strength.drivers: CPI 4.2% headline / core 2.9% (May); June NFP +57k big miss (unemp 4.2%); Fed 3.50-3.75% hawkish hold, SEP 1-2 hikes, zero 2026 cuts. DXY firm ~101.1 on higher-for-longer, but the war-shock haven premium is deflating (gold faded, oil capped) so the bid is now rates, not fear.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ Firm into the Jul 28-29 FOMC, but Jul 14 CPI is the reset: headline soft (-0.1% m/m -> ~3.9%) vs core firm (+0.3%, 2.9%). Risk: a soft headline misread dovish cracks DXY under 100.5; a hot core re-fires the no-cut trade. Next FOMC Jul 28-29.

strength.tag: services CPI 3.7%; own rate story as risk-off fades
strength.drivers: Core 2.6% (May); services CPI 3.7% (up from 3.2%); headline 2.8% held; DMP wages 4.2%. BoE 3.75%, hawkish 7-2 hold (2 hike votes). With risk-off fading GBP trades its own firm rate story again rather than as a risk passenger.
strength.forward: 🦅 Services re-acceleration keeps the hike tail and both dissents alive into Jul 30. Risk: a hot US CPI drags cable via the dollar; UK June CPI (~Jul 22) is the domestic check. Next BoE Jul 30.

strength.tag: WTI ~$74 holds USD/CAD off ~1.42; Oman cap
strength.drivers: May CPI 3.2% (energy-driven; ex-energy 2.1%, trim ~2.0%); Jun jobs +18.2k beat (unemp 6.5% - but all part-time, mfg -17k); BoC 2.25%, 5th straight hold; technical recession. Oil ~$74 hands CAD a modest terms-of-trade bid, but the Oman two-corridor proposal caps the upside.
strength.forward: ⚖️🦅 The oil bid holds USD/CAD off ~1.42 but is modest, not the Sunday supply-shock; BoC sidelined Jul 15, internals hollow. Risk: an Oman deal crushes oil and the CAD bid; a hot US CPI re-fires USD/CAD. Next BoC Jul 15.

strength.drivers: RBNZ hiked 25bp to 2.50% Jul 8, 6-0, but scripted dovish: headline inflation seen PEAKED at 3.9% Q2 (easing to 3.3% Q3), a 'considerably higher hurdle' for the next move. Soft China CPI (+1%). With risk-off fading, the high-beta down-gap threat recedes.
strength.forward: ⚖️🔻 The dovish-hike floor stands and the tape has steadied; Aug 19 should be a hold. NZ Q2 CPI (~Jul 16) validates or breaks the 'peaked at 3.9%' framing. Risk: a fresh geopolitics leg re-hits NZD; a 4%+ print re-arms the hawks. Next RBNZ Aug 19.

strength.tag: claws back risk-beta loss; thin Aug tail (~36%)
strength.drivers: May CPI: trimmed mean 3.6%; headline 4.0%; unemp 4.5% loosening (June jobs Jul 16). RBA 4.35% (held Jun after 75bp of 2026 hikes); Aug 11 hike priced only ~36%. China CPI missed at +1%. As the war-shock fades AUD recovers the Sunday risk-off leg, lightly helped by LNG-export status.
strength.forward: 🔻⚖️ Sticky-core floor; the tape steadying lets AUD firm. Risk: a geopolitics re-escalation re-softens it; a durable calm + a hot Q2 CPI (late Jul) re-arms the hawks. Next RBA Aug 11.

strength.verdict: SOFT — energy hit easing but low
strength.drivers: EZ June flash HICP 2.8% (down from 3.2%) BUT core ROSE to 2.5% from 2.2%. Unemp 6.3%. ECB 2.25% depo (hiked Jun 11), meeting-by-meeting. EUR is still the energy-importer laggard at a 1-yr low (~1.14) vs a firm USD, though the capped oil eases the acute terms-of-trade hit.
strength.forward: 🔻⚖️ Oil-inflation lifts Sept-hike bets but stays a slow forward story, not a spot bid; the firm USD leads near term. Risk: a soft US CPI relieves it; a hawkish Jul 23 core tone hands EUR a leg. Next ECB Jul 23.

strength.verdict: WEAK — the haven bounce never came
strength.tag: spot ~162; carry re-grinds toward the MoF zone
strength.drivers: Core CPI 1.4% (May, subsidy-suppressed); unemp 2.5%, jobs/applicants 1.26 tight. BoJ 1.00% (hiked Jun 16, 7-1), guides more hikes. The pivotal call resolved against the yen: risk-off faded and gold sold, so no haven bounce - carry + Japan's ~100% energy-import bill re-grind USD/JPY back toward ~162-163. MoF verbal-only, Y11.73tn precedent unused.
strength.forward: ⚖️🦅 Carry re-grinds it up toward 162-163 and the MoF zone; BoJ Jul 31 hawkish. Risk: a geopolitics re-escalation finally triggers the haven bid; a >163 grind forces the MoF's hand. Next BoJ Jul 31.

symbols.risk: An Oman deal crushes oil and the CAD bid; a hot US CPI re-fires the long.

symbols.why: Two-way now: a contested-Strait / traffic-standstill floor (~$74) vs an Oman two-corridor cap - the Sunday supply-shock premium is deflating.
symbols.risk: An Oman deal signed unwinds it fast; the stacked Iran+Saudi glut caps the top; a re-escalation re-spikes it.

symbols.why: The haven bounce never came, so carry + Japan's energy bill re-grind it up toward 162-163; intervention risk near 163 caps the top.
symbols.risk: A geopolitics re-escalation finally triggers a yen haven bid; a soft US CPI caps it.

symbols.why: Firm hawkish GBP vs a firm USD into CPI - the dollar leads the near-term move.
symbols.risk: A hot US CPI presses it under 1.34; a hawkish UK CPI (~Jul 22) re-firms GBP.

symbols.why: Firm ~101.1 on higher-for-longer, but the haven premium is fading (gold sold), so the bid is rates; CPI Jul 14 is the reset.

symbols.why: The war-shock haven bounce never came, so the basket stays soft (opposite ticket to XXX/JPY) as weak-JPY carry re-grinds.

symbols.risk: A high-beta flush if a re-escalation revives growth fear; follows gold if the haven leg re-fires.

catalysts.note: The dominant near-term driver: headline seen soft (-0.1% m/m -> ~3.9% on June's lower gasoline) but core firm (+0.3%, ~2.9%) - a split print, a genuine two-way for the dollar.
Southern (Omani-waters) lane free; Northern (Iranian-waters) lane open but needs Iran approval - a signed deal crushes the oil/haven premium; a breakdown re-spikes it.

catalysts.event: Strait of Hormuz - IRGC 'closed' vs CENTCOM 'open'
catalysts.note: 4th US strike Sun; ~6,000 seafarers stranded, traffic near-standstill - every headline moves oil, CAD, JPY, gold, risk.
Hold; capped-oil bid vs hollow internals + technical recession.
RBNZ says headline PEAKED at 3.9% - the print validates or breaks the 'higher hurdle' guidance.
Unemployment 4.5% loosening; feeds the Aug 11 RBA calculus.
Hold; core-2.5% + oil-inflation is the Sept-hike signal; capped oil eases the dovish complication.
Warsh round 2; hold + hawkish minimalism; CPI + oil frame the message.
Dissent count for GBP after services 3.7%.
Guidance with spot ~162 and the MoF zone hot; no haven relief for the yen.
June-17 MoU's negotiating window ends; Hormuz administration the core dispute.

geopolitics: The Sunday escalation is real but the panic is fading - diplomacy is back in play. The US ran its 4th strike in a week against Iran on Sunday, retaliating for the IRGC's attack on the Cyprus-flagged container ship GFS Galaxy, and the Strait remains a live dispute - the IRGC still calls it 'closed until further notice' while CENTCOM insists it 'is open... Iran does not control the strait.' Traffic is at a near-standstill with ~6,000 seafarers stranded aboard hundreds of vessels. But the escalation did NOT compound into Monday: Iran's FM Araghchi is in active mediation with Oman, which has drafted a concrete two-corridor proposal - a Southern Corridor through Omani territorial waters offering free navigation under pre-war conditions, and a Northern Corridor through Iranian waters that stays open but requires Iran's prior approval (no tolls). Trump signalled the truce talks continue even as the strikes landed. That off-ramp is why the market gapped but did not extend. FX transmission (Monday, gap digested): oil did its job on the open - WTI ~$74 (+5.4% on the week) - but the Oman cap and truce talks have turned it two-way; CAD keeps a modest terms-of-trade bid that holds USD/CAD off ~1.42; the USD is firm (DXY ~101.1) but the bid rotated from haven to rates into CPI; gold is the tell - it FAILED the war-shock bid and fell ~1.2% to ~$4,070, the clearest sign the fear premium is unwinding; EUR stays the energy-importer laggard at a 1-yr low but the capped oil eases the acute hit; AUD/NZD claw back their risk-beta losses; GER40 gets a reprieve; silver is torn between a fading haven leg and a firming industrial leg. The yen is the resolved call: the risk-off haven bounce never arrived, so carry + Japan's ~100% energy-import bill re-grind USD/JPY back toward ~162-163 and the MoF zone. The complex is now CPI-led with a geopolitics tail: Tuesday's split print drives the dollar, while any breakdown in the Oman talks can re-arm the oil/haven trade in minutes - which is why the board carries no high-conviction pairs into the print.
